title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Gone from not working at all, too perfect 60fps at highest settings on no GPU overclock. Anyone struggling to get a game to work, update your kernel to the bleeding edge. That solved everything for me on my main rig (this hardware). I even went as far as to reinstall Ubuntu, which didn't work, only after a kernel update. Game doesn't feel quite as smooth as running native, though it's not going to stop me playing all the way through on Ubuntu

title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes:
MP: Joined an empty multiplayer server to try out sound, graphics, movement and shooting. Seems to work just fine.
SP: Played through the first/prelude mission, went fine. Mouse speed extremely slow in the boat scene, but fine afterwards. Watched the first cutscene in the next mission "Cold war". Minor stuttering (might be building shaders etc), but works fine otherwise.
Menus: All works fine, except for the mouse cursor/pointer which flickers, making it hard to spot sometimes.
Other: Game crashed when I tried to play a game with my old soldier/save created when playing the game through EA's Origin client. Created a new soldier on my EA account and it worked fine.

title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Out of the box, Steam Play did not launch the game. To force the game to load with DX11, I had to add PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command% to the launch options. To force DX9, you can add PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%. With DX9, I was able to use gallium-nine with excellent performance. With DX11 (OpenGL-based, not the Vulkan-based DXVK), the textures didn't load properly on the main cut scene. You can likely setup the DX11 to use DXVK, but it's not currently configured by Steam and will require manual labor.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes:
For multiplayer: If you bought the game from Steam, you'll need to first sign into Origin, click Game Library on the left side, and add BF: BC2 and paste in the CD KEY that Steam provides you, then you'll have no problems logging in and playing.

Also, update to my previous post: Definitely use DX11 instead of DX9. On a fresh Steam install, if the game doesn't start just add PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 to force DX11. Once you're in the game, if you get black, blocky textures, you can be fix this by toggling between the different MSAA settings until fixed. If that doesn't work, you'll need newer Mesa 18.3+ drivers (for AMD cards). Game runs great after these two tweaks.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Latest update: I'm back to using DX9 over DX11. I'm finding DX11 to be way too inconsistent and the textures don't always load properly, at least on my AMD card. So to recap, for DX9 use the launch option: PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Running perfectly, may occur when installing the game does not start, in this case recommend to add the following command in the game startup options. "PROTON_NO_D3D11 = 1% command%" is perfectly stable, sometimes momentary crashes occur. but I think it's because my Intel HD only has 256 MB and I'm not using any other cards, I'll leave a screenshot of one of the missions of the game. https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1575791572

cpu: AMD A12-9720P RADEON R7, 12 COMPUTE CORES 4C+8G
notes: The reason I picked Borked is because currently the game is stuck at the serial key popup, which is preventing me from playing the game. When that popup comes up, the game asks for me to type in the serial key. After doing that, the game will claim a connection error occurred and then I'm stuck into a loop where I choose Retry and then it keeps asking for a serial key. I can't get past that and try the gameplay any further. In other words, this is a gameplay-breaking bug, and that's why I chose to go with Borked.

title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: At first, the game refused to launch; however, adding PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% to the Set Launch Options in the game properties window enabled the game to start. Thanks to others for posting this fix. Single player campaign works without issue. Although I could create a new EA account within BFBC2, I could not enter the game key to unlock multiplayer. To enable multiplayer, I went to https://www.ea.com and logged into my newly created account --> Account Settings --> Redeem Product Code and entered my BFBC2 product code. I was then able to launch BFBC2, enter my EA account info, and play multiplayer.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Game does not run for me on anything newer than Proton 3.7.8. If I use anything newer than 3.7.8 it runs worse and has an invisible cursor bug. Runs fine on 3.7.8 with no issues.
